issue_title: RPL_MYINFO / RPL_ISUPPORT needed for chanmodes with parameters instead of hardcoded "bklohvq"
issue_body:
A note first: this ticket is based on taking a look at https://github.com/jaraco/irc/blob/master/irc/modes.py
If that is outdated/unused code, this ticket is probably invalid and should be ignored.

It appears that the irclib doesn't correctly handle MODE parsing: it seems to assume a hardcoded set of "bklvohq" for channel modes with parameters. However, this can lead to all sorts of problems with modern IRC daemons that introduce additional modes.

The correct behavior would be:

1. Instead of the current single hardcoded set "bklvohq", always use two whereas one contains the modes that have parameters when they are added (+), and the other modes that have parameters when removed(-) in a MODE event.

2. Check 005 RPL_iSUPPORT for the CHANMODES=.... parameter. (see http://www.irc.org/tech_docs/005.html ) The set of channel modes with parameters should be set to A, B, C (see link) for modes that are added, and A, B for modes that are removed.

3. If there's no CHANMODES in 005, assume 004 RPL_MYINFO numeric syntax `<server> <ver> <usermode> <chanmode> <chanmodes with parameters> ... `     (the <chanmodes with parameters> parameter and possible further additional ones are not in the original spec, but added by 99% of modern IRC servers - also see here https://www.alien.net.au/irc/irc2numerics.html ) and extract `<chanmodes with parameters>`. Use this set both for modes that are added and removed in a MODE event (that is an approximation, but you can't do any better). 

4. Use the two newly introduced sets and only use "bklvohq" as a fallback when parsing MODE events.

**Important: this requires a "welcome" event delay:**
(this might already present, I am only adding this in case it isn't)
Please note this approach will not work correctly in all cases and run into a possible race condition unless you do the following:

1. Wait for 004
2. Wait for 005 with a timeout (the timeout is needed to support legacy servers without RPL_ISUPPORT). 
3. Extract the info as described above out of 004 contents and possibly 005 if it was observed
4. Only then trigger a "welcome" / connected event to the library user

Otherwise, the library user might join a channel and observe a mode event before the 004 / 005 have been received. (race condition)

The socket read is run through the JSON

>     def refresh(self, gpsd_data_package):
        """Sets new socket data as Fix attributes
        Arguments:
            self:
            gpsd_data_package (json object):
        Provides:
        self attribute dictionaries, e.g., self.TPV['lat'], self.SKY['gdop']
        Raises:
        AttributeError: 'str' object has no attribute 'keys' when the device falls out of the system
        ValueError, KeyError: most likely extra, or mangled JSON data, should not happen, but that
        applies to a lot of things.
        """
        try:
            fresh_data = json.loads(gpsd_data_package)  # The reserved word 'class' is popped from JSON object class
            package_name = fresh_data.pop('class', 'ERROR')  # gpsd data package errors are also 'ERROR'.
            package = getattr(self, package_name, package_name)  # packages are named for JSON object class
            for key in package.keys():  # TODO: Rollover and retry.  It fails here when device disappears
                package[key] = fresh_data.get(key, 'n/a')  # Updates and restores 'n/a' if key is absent in the socket
                # response, present --> 'key: 'n/a'' instead.'
        except AttributeError:  # 'str' object has no attribute 'keys'
            print('No Data')
            return
        except (ValueError, KeyError) as error:
            sys.stderr.write(str(error))  # Look for extra data in stream
            return


As you see this last ValueError, KeyError throw a error with extraneous data that is in the JSON object shipped from GPSD

issue_title: Plim doesn't respect mako's ## comments properly.
issue_body:
I know that Plim has it's own comment feature ( / ) but ignoring Mako's commenting symbols ( ## ) means that you have to change the mako files to work with plim. Normally, that's no big deal, since plim supports its own versions of what mako does, and in your documents, you can just use plim's / But it means that we can't put comments in inherited or namespaced files, which may NOT be plim files, but are "pure" mako files. For example, I have a helpers.mako file that contains some things I want to pull into other documents/templates. So far, so good; I can grab these through standard Mako tools, either in pure mako or in plim. But if I want to put a comment in that PURE mako file, plim doesn't respect it. The below will output both sets of text if plim is used as a pre-processor on documents that inherit this block and use it. It seems to want to make a div with the id "#" which is non-sensical (and perhaps invalid html) but makes sense according to the #myid use case. ``` <%block name="something_to_use_elsewhere">

This should be here.

## But this should not. ``` renders as ``` <%block name="something_to_use_elsewhere">

This should be here.

But this should not.
``` And if I change the ## into a / to suit plim, then I have invalid mako, and other code can't use it. Well, not invalid, but again, it's not interpreted as a comment. What's weird is that in the docs here http://plim.readthedocs.org/en/latest/en/syntax.html#new-style-blocks it uses a # to make a comment in the generated mako code, but not at the beginning of a line. I don't see anyplace where ## is already used by plim, can we make it either pass through, or ... something? Losing `
` doesn't seem so bad ;-) tl;dr, can we have mako comments back?
See #46.

Would it make more sense to return outputs directly for indicators returning a single output? Indicators returning multiple outputs could use a tuple.

This way, you can easily pass indicator outputs directly to other indicators. For example, a moving average crossover strategy is simply:

```Python
buy = ti.crossover(ti.sma(close,5), ti.sma(close,24))
```

instead of:

```Python
buy = ti.crossover(ti.sma(close,5)['sma'], ti.sma(close,24)['sma'])['sma']
```

There are only 8 indicators that return multiple outputs, so I think that this would be optimizing for the major use case.

Using tuples for multiple outputs seems easier too:

```Python
lower, middle, upper = ti.bbands(close, 14.0, 1.0)
```

I've implemented the change in [this branch](https://github.com/cirla/tulipy/tree/return).

Looks like the `Faker` namespace on Pypi has been abandoned for quite a while: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/Faker

This project's owner @joke2k should file a request to have the namespace transferred to this project. 

It would reduce confusion between Faker (project name) and `fake-factory` (how it gets pip installed). 

The pypi team is pretty good about transferring namespaces if they're clearly abandoned (which seems to be the case here).

Not a bug, just an improvement.

HTTP headers are returned unordered (they are held in a simple dict). Simple patch to make headers be returned in the same order as they are in the request.

This is helpful for the very particular use case to fingerprint certain browsers.

This does have some drawbacks, since OrderedDicts are python 2.7 and above only. Backwards compatibility can be achieved using ordereddict, but it introduces an extra dependency.

**Attachment:** [ordered_http_headers.patch](http://code.google.com/p/dpkt/issues/detail?id=138)

_Original issue: http://code.google.com/p/dpkt/issues/detail?id=138_

issue_title: `Constraints` should not be a seperate type.
issue_body:
`Constraint`'s are fundamentally just `Model`'s and so should not have a seperate role. They were introduced mostly because they should be derived w.r.t. all the parameters of the associated model, not of the constraint itself.

However, at the time of coding this I had forgotten that python is a consenting adults language. So we could just initiate a normal `Model` with the expression of the constraint, and then overwrite its `.params` and `.vars` with those of the associated model.

This does leave the issue of what to do about the two different fundamental types: `Eq` or `GreaterThan`. This could be either set as an attribute on the model, or we track the different types in the calling function (`Fit`).

issue_title: Incompatibility between pygrib latlons() and NDFD oceanic GRIB2 files
issue_body:
I have encountered an incompatibility issue between pygrib latlons() and NDFD oceanic grids (GRIB2 files). The longitudes do not seem to be read in correctly using latlons(). It seems that the result of the command is that longitudes range from 129.906005 to 10.71. The longitudes should range from 129.906005 East to 10.71 East, but contain either negative longitudes or longitudes greater than 180 to indicate degrees West.

In other words, the minimum and maximum longitudes using latlons() is 10.71 and 129.906005, respectively, when they really should range from either -180 to 180 or 0 to 360 depending on the convention used for longitude.

The below few lines provide an example to reproduce the error. You can download an example NDFD oceanic GRIB2 file here: http://tgftp.nws.noaa.gov/SL.us008001/ST.expr/DF.gr2/DC.ndfd/AR.oceanic/VP.001-003/

`import pygrib`
`#GRIB2 file downloaded from http://tgftp.nws.noaa.gov/SL.us008001/ST.expr/DF.gr2/DC.ndfd/AR.oceanic/VP.001-003/, which is from https://para.ocean.weather.gov/opc_gridded_marine.php:`
`grib='ds.wspd.bin';`
`grbs=pygrib.open(grib)`
`spd10m  = grbs.select(shortName='ws',typeOfLevel='surface',level=0)[0].values`
`lat,lon = grbs[1].latlons()`
`print lon[800][800]`
`print lat[800][800]`
`print lon.min(),lon.max(),lat.min(),lat.max()`

The result of the above script outputs the following for me:

`92.0058444277
40.7152444663
10.71 129.906005 -30.4192 80.01`

For comparison, wgrib2 outputs the following:

`[opc_test@vm-lnx7-opctest2: /common/data/products/NDFD/grib2/exp/oceanic_day1to3]> wgrib2 -d 1 -ijlat 800 800 ds.wspd.bin
1:80:(800,800),lon=206.377544,lat=40.642622,val=9.999e+20
[opc_test@vm-lnx7-opctest2: /common/data/products/NDFD/grib2/exp/oceanic_day1to3]>`

So, longitude at the (800,800) grid point is 206.377544 using wgrib2 (longitudes range from 0 to 360), whereas pygrib's latlons() shows a longitude of 92.0058444277 at (800,800) (longitudes decrease/range from 129.906005 to 10.71).

Using wgrib2 to convert to netcdf (wgrib2 -netcdf) correctly outputs longitudes ranging from 0 to 360.

Initial response received is that running the utility 'grib_list' on one of those files, you'll get:

`latitudeOfFirstGridPointInDegrees = -30.4192
longitudeOfFirstGridPointInDegrees = 129.906005
latitudeOfLastGridPointInDegrees = 80.01
longitudeOfLastGridPointInDegrees = 10.71`

The potential problem is that the domain crosses the Greenwich meridian, and the code does not work correctly when the longitudes are not monotonically increasing.

It seems that either the metadata in the file could be incorrect, or the grib_api C library could be interpreting it incorrectly.

Thanks,
Greg

issue_title: Fix typo for autompg and wine data
issue_body:
I noticed a couple (very minor) typos in the wine and autompg dataset documentation. In the example on the wine dataset documentation there is the following line:
```
print('\nHeader: %s' % ['sepal length', 'sepal width',
                        'petal length', 'petal width'])
```
These are the features for the iris dataset, not the wine dataset. In addition, in the autompg there is a feature listed as `'horsepower weight'`, but should be two separate features `'horsepower', 'weight'`

issue_body: Is there a way to identify if the md5 of the html is the same as during previous crawl and ignore further processing, so that we don't re-parse what's already been parsed if html contains no differences based on md5 checksum?

issue_title: Allow setting or_else() value in advance
issue_body:
For some of my work, I'm considering exposing some objects as Maybe objects instead of just dict like objects. In some specific cases I'll need to provide and or_else() value to the user so he or she doesn't have to set it him or herself.

We could imagine something like:

    (test) >>> print(maybe('foo').bar.or_else())
    None
    (test) >>> print(maybe('foo', or_else="wololo").bar.or_else())
    wololo

This way I can even abstract this part of the process, but still allow a user to override it if needed.

What do you think ?

issue_title: Finnish fixed holidays are moved to the next monday if they occur on the weekend
issue_body:
I'm using calendra 1.1.3 on Python 2.7.8. I'm using the Finnish holiday calendar and have noticed a problem with it.

When using the Finnish calendar, fixed holidays that occur on a weekend are moved to the next working day. Example, the Finnish Independence Day that is the 6th of December:

```
In [3]: holiday_calendar.is_observed_holiday(date(2014, 12, 8))                                                       
Out[3]: True

In [4]: holiday_calendar.is_observed_holiday(date(2014, 12, 6))                                                       
Out[4]: False

In [7]: holiday_calendar.get_observed_date(date(2014, 12, 6))                                                         
Out[7]: datetime.date(2014, 12, 8)
```

This also happens with the Labour Day (1st of May). But in Finland the day off is not transferred to the next monday if the day occurs on the weekend. I can't find a source on it though, but you can check other calendars such as http://www.timeanddate.com/holidays/finland/.

This issue can be fixed by setting an `observance_shift = dict()` for the Finnish calendar, though I arrived at the value of `dict()` just by random testing (I don't know the format) and I don't know if it has any adverse effects.

Hi All, 

I've encountered a situation where the Winston-Lutz optimization didn't act as expected, and on further investigation, I wanted to discuss with the wider community. 

I gave the algorithm four images at cardinal gantry angles (0, 90, 180, 270) from an old linac with a radiation isocentre diameter in the 1-2mm range. The G0 and G180 images gave CAX positions which differed relative to the BB by ~1.2mm, and the lateral images differed by ~0.5mm. 

The algorithm works by minimizing the maximum distance from the radiation isocentre point to any line it finds. Given four images like that, once the radiation isocentre point is located between the two projections with the larger distance between them, there is nothing to push the optimization to minimize the distance to the projections with smaller distances between them - in my case the algorithm essentially ignored these projections. See the image below - the red dot is the calculated radiation isocentre, the blue cross is the BB, the red and orange lines are the AP projections and the green and blue lines are the lateral projections. 

![wl](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/943852/29117930-f976b4fa-7cf7-11e7-81d1-04ec1f6d4fb2.png)

Would it be more appropriate if the minimization was looking to reduce the average distance?

Both `_read_channel()` and `_read_channel_expect()` do not check for EOF after calling `self.remote_conn.recv()`.  This causes a connection which has closed to raise

`NetMikoTimeoutException("Timed-out reading channel, pattern not found in output: {}".format(pattern))`

This isn't exactly desired behavior as it makes it hard to discern whether the command just took too long or the connection is dead.  Most of the time, a subsequent call to `self.send_command()` will cause paramiko to raise `socket.error("Socket is closed")`, but I have ran into a case where the socket only half closed, so the `socket.error` never got raised.

I can fix this, but I'd like guidance on the preferred way to signal this error to the user.  `EOFError`?  A netmiko exception?  Something else?

Code using _pslinux.py has the tendency to report not-so-useful errors such as:

```
/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/psutil/_pslinux.py:1153: RuntimeWarning: ignoring IOError(2, 'No such file or directory')
  warnings.warn("ignoring %r" % err, RuntimeWarning)
```

... which doesn't tell us which filename has caused the error.

The patch below does two things: firstly it tells us the filename causing the error to occur, and secondly (and this logic may need to be confirmed) it only looks for nodes with an '_input' element to their name:

```
@@ -1134,11 +1134,11 @@
       difficult to parse
     """
     ret = collections.defaultdict(list)
-    basenames = glob.glob('/sys/class/hwmon/hwmon*/temp*_*')
+    basenames = glob.glob('/sys/class/hwmon/hwmon*/temp*_input')
     # CentOS has an intermediate /device directory:
     # https://github.com/giampaolo/psutil/issues/971
     # https://github.com/nicolargo/glances/issues/1060
-    basenames.extend(glob.glob('/sys/class/hwmon/hwmon*/device/temp*_*'))
+    basenames.extend(glob.glob('/sys/class/hwmon/hwmon*/device/temp*_input'))
     basenames = sorted(set([x.split('_')[0] for x in basenames]))
 
     for base in basenames:
@@ -1150,7 +1150,7 @@
             # https://github.com/giampaolo/psutil/issues/1009
             # https://github.com/giampaolo/psutil/issues/1101
             # https://github.com/giampaolo/psutil/issues/1129
-            warnings.warn("ignoring %r for file '%s'" % (err, RuntimeWarning), base)
+            warnings.warn(("ignoring %r for file '%s'" % (err, base + '_input')), RuntimeWarning)
             continue
 
         unit_name = cat(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(base), 'name'),

issue_title: ValueError: size must be a divisor of the total size in bytes of the last axis of the array
issue_body:
I have a numpy array with dimensions (4, 364, 3969), and try to convert it into a quat_array, e.g.,  


`quat.as_quat_array(np.random.rand(4, 364, 3969) )`

and get the error

```
------------------------------------------------------------
ValueError                 Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-35-79d845f77eba> in <module>()
----> 1 quat.as_quat_array(testarray)

~/miniconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/quaternion/__init__.py in as_quat_array(a)
     90     if not a.flags['C_CONTIGUOUS'] or a.strides[-1] != a.itemsize:
     91         a = a.copy(order='C')
---> 92     av = a.view(np.quaternion)
     93 
     94     # special case - don't create an axis for a single quaternion, to match

ValueError: When changing to a larger dtype, its size must be a divisor of the total size in bytes of the last axis of the array.
```


Interestingly, 

`quat.as_quat_array(np.random.rand(4, 364, 3968) )` works, `(4, 364, 3964)` does also, `quat.as_quat_array(np.random.rand(4, 364, 3967) )` does not, so  it seems that the last dimension must ba divisible by 4. 

The same applies for larger arrays, e.g.

`quat.as_quat_array(np.random.rand(4, 364, 3964,16) )` works

`quat.as_quat_array(np.random.rand(4, 364, 3964,15) )` does not.


The workaround would probably be to design only arrays with last dimensions divisible by 4 (as the error suggests, "its size must be a divisor of the total size in bytes of the last axis of the array.").

Is there another way to handle this behavior? Thank you!

issue_title: Codec tests fail with new lz4-python release (0.12.0)

issue_body:
Hello, I have a question about the `contains_rva` function in the `SectionStructure` (https://github.com/erocarrera/pefile/blob/master/pefile.py#L1098)

> Check whether there's any section after the current one that starts before the calculated end for the current one, if so, cut the current section's size to fit in the range up to where the next section starts.

Would there be a valid executable file (eg the Windows loader accepts it) that has this property or is it to properly handle malformed files?

The corkami PE wiki (https://corkamiwiki.github.io/PE#Misc_VirtualSize) doesn't appear to say anything about it, it only says (related):

> A section can have a null VirtualSize: in this case, only the SizeOfRawData is taken into consideration.
> - `nullvirt` has a section with a null VirtualSize and a non-null SizeOfRawData.

issue_title: No results when VARCHAR(MAX) exceeds 8k characters on MS SQL
issue_body:
If a ``VARCHAR(MAX)`` exceeds the 8k characters, ``pyodbc`` does not return any result if ``unicode_results=True`` on MS SQL (``driver='{SQL Server Native Client 11.0}'``).

That is,
```python
>>> sql = "SELECT CAST(REPLICATE('x', 8000) AS VARCHAR(MAX)) + CAST(REPLICATE('x', 8000) AS VARCHAR(MAX)) AS txt";
>>> cursor = conn.execute(sql);
>>> results = cursor.fetchall()
[]
```
Using ``NVARCHAR(MAX)`` does return the correct result.
```python
>>> sql = "SELECT CAST(REPLICATE('x', 8000) AS NVARCHAR(MAX)) + CAST(REPLICATE('x', 8000) AS NVARCHAR(MAX)) AS txt";
```
If ``unicode_results=False``, it also returns the correct result for ``VARCHAR(MAX)``.

issue_title: Issues with UTF-16 strings on 64 bit versions
issue_body:
I know I commented on this issue back on google code, but never received a response. https://code.google.com/p/pyodbc/issues/detail?id=78

Basically it looks like it was "fixed" at some point, and then the patch reverted some time around 2014. I checked this new github repository, and the problematic line still appears to be there:

https://github.com/mkleehammer/pyodbc/blob/master/src/getdata.cpp#L319


You can glean plenty of details from that first link, but I'll quote some in case google code disappears some day:

```
What steps will reproduce the problem?
1 Setup odbc correctly.
2 Execute the following:
 import pyodbc
 database = pyodbc.connect('DSN=localhost')
 cursor = database.cursor()
 cursor.execute('SHOW CREATE TABLE mysql.users')
 print cursor.fetchall()
 cursor.close()
 database.close()
2 Notice the output being in UTF-16 in memory format, containing something
like this:
 [(u'\U00730075\U00720065', u'\U00520043\U00410045\U00450054\U00540020 ...

What is the expected output? What do you see instead?
Something readable in UTF-8, containing something like this:
...
`Host` char(60) COLLATE utf8_bin NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
`User` char(16) COLLATE utf8_bin NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
...

What version of the product are you using? On what operating system?
pyodbc-2.1.5-2.fc11.i586
unixODBC-2.2.14-2.fc11.i586
mysql-5.1.37-1.fc11.i586
mysql-libs-5.1.37-1.fc11.i586
mysql-server-5.1.37-1.fc11.i586
mysql-connector-odbc-5.1.5r1144-4.fc11.i586
```

```
I've had the same issue, which only seems to happen on 64-bit machines.

Here's my setup:
ubuntu 10.4 64-bit
pyodbc-2.1.7
unixODBC-2.2.11-21
freeTDS-0.82

Server:
MS SQL Server 2008

I've traced the problem back to a call to SQLGetData in getdata.cpp. The function doesn't seem to handle unicode strings correcty on 64-bit systems. Specifying a charset in the pyodbc.connect() call doesn't fix it. I was able to fix it by mapping all unicode character types to SQL_C_CHAR, I'm attaching a diff of the fix.

Daniel Erickson
Concentric Sky, Inc.
www.concentricsky.com
```

Contents of patch:

```
diff --git a/src/getdata.cpp b/src/getdata.cpp
index a487ae3..660116b 100644
--- a/src/getdata.cpp
+++ b/src/getdata.cpp
@@ -262,7 +262,8 @@ GetDataString(Cursor* cur, int iCol)
     case SQL_WCHAR:
     case SQL_WVARCHAR:
     case SQL_WLONGVARCHAR:
-        nTargetType  = SQL_C_WCHAR;
+        //nTargetType  = SQL_C_WCHAR;
+        nTargetType  = SQL_C_CHAR;
         break;
         
     default:
```

issue_title: module available, but getting py3status: loading module failed?
issue_body:
Sorry if this is not the appropriate place, but I can't find a support list or forum?  I'm trying to add a status for mpc to my bar.  I looked at:

$ py3status modules list
  mpd_status             Display information from mpd.

So I assume it's "installed" and available.  I added the following to my .i3status.conf:

order += "mpdstatus"
and
mpdstatus {
        name = "♬"
        cache_timeout = 0
        host = "localhost"
        port = 6600
}

per http://py3status-modules.readthedocs.org/en/latest/configuration.html?#id4

but every time I reload with this module, I get a problem

loading module "mpdstatus" failed (No module named mpdstatus)

error in i3 when I reload.  I figured I missed a step of `py3status modules install mpd_status` or something, but the docs here:
https://github.com/ultrabug/py3status/wiki/Load-and-order-py3status-modules-directly-from-your-current-i3status-config

simply state I should be able to use the name of the module without any special installation?  I've tried using mpd_status as the module name, but then I get an error about missing the `mpd` module.

What am I missing?

On OpenBSD (6.2-current), py3status fails with an error:
```
file: unknown option -- mime-encoding
usage: file [-bchiLsW] file ...
```
The `file` command on OpenBSD does not have the `--mime-encoding` option. The command is used in: `parse_config.py` ine 536
```
encoding = check_output(['file', '-b', '--mime-encoding', '--dereference', config_path])
encoding = encoding.strip().decode('utf-8')
```
As a workaround, I replaced those lines with hardcoded value:
```
encoding = 'utf-8'
```
then the program runs OK.

Maybe encoding guess should use a python library (chardet?) instead of using the file command. I will try to propose a patch (never coded anything in python, but this is a nice way to learn).

issue_title: Support for additional claims in create_jwt_token method
issue_body:
First, thanks for publishing this package. Like you, I was using [pyramid_jwtauth](https://github.com/ajkavanagh/pyramid_jwtauth). This is a much cleaner implementation!

I need to include additional claims (e.g. group, role) in the payload, which ideally I'd like to be able to include like...

```python
token = request.create_jwt_token(claims)
```

...instead of having to define an additional method...

```python
import jwt
from pyramid.interfaces import IAuthenticationPolicy

def encode_auth_token(request, claims):
    policy = request.registry.queryUtility(IAuthenticationPolicy)
    return jwt.encode(claims, policy.private_key, algorithm=policy.algorithm)

token = encode_auth_token(request, claims)
```

(Of course, this doesn't include any default claims like your current `create_jwt_token` method.)

I'd suggest that additional claims are common enough to warrant including in the convenience method attached to the request object. If I submitted a PR, would you rather see the inclusion of additional claims as an update to the `create_jwt_token` method or as a new method altogether (e.g. `create_custom_jwt_token`)? If an update to `create_jwt_token` is preferable, could it be a breaking change like...

```python
token = request.create_jwt_token(claims)
```

...or should it be a non-breaking (but more confusing) change like...

```python
token = request.create_jwt_token(user_id, claims)
```
?

Thanks.

issue_title: dateparser throws TypeError error with specific number combo
issue_body:
If datefinder attempts to parse a string such as "32 2016", it throws an error "TypeError: Required argument 'day' (pos 3) not found".

The combination needs to be a string with a combination of 2 and 4 digits, and the number with 2 digits needs to be greater than 31 or less than 1 (eg 00) to throw the error.

I found myself deep down the dateparser rabbit hole, but thought this scenario may need to be filtered in datefinder prior to passing to dateparser.parse. Eg something like the below added to parse_date_string (this is a bit ugly, but does the job)

```
date_string_split = date_string.split()
if len(date_string_split) == 2:
    if len(date_string_split[0]) == 2 and len(date_string_split[1]) == 4 and (int(date_string_split[0]) < 1 or int(date_string_split[0]) > 31):
            return None
    if len(date_string_split[1]) == 2 and len(date_string_split[0]) == 4 and (int(date_string_split[1]) < 1 or int(date_string_split[1]) > 31):
            return None
```

issue_body: I had a situation where 2 fields were on the same row, I checked the css and I saw that one of them had a class called field-box just because the field name was box, so I solved it reming the field, but it would be better to use a different naming convention to prevent that this could happen.

Right now `InitVar[something] is InitVar`. This means that any type-information is removed, and it will (for instance) be impossible to do type info checks on the generated `__init__`, or find out anything about the type of the variable.

What is the rationale for using the class `__name__` instead of `__qualname__` in a dataclass `__repr__`?
It seems like using `__qualname__` would keep nesting visible (the default for classes since PEP3155) without sacrificing succinctness for top-level classes.

Example to illustrate:

```python
@dataclass
class A:
     pass

class B:
    @dataclass
    class C:
        pass

def factory():
    @dataclass
    class D:
        pass
    return D
D = factory()
```

with `__name__` (current situation):

```python
>>> A()
A()
>>> B.C()
C()
>>> D()
D()
```

with `__qualname__`:

```python
>>> A()
A()
>>> B.C()
B.C()
>>> D()
factory.<locals>.D()
```

serverless-wsgi currently sets the `REMOTE_ADDR` variable like this:

~~~
        'REMOTE_ADDR':
            headers.get(u'X-Forwarded-For', '').split(', ')[0],
~~~

But that's insecure, since anyone can submit a request with their own `X-Forwarded-For:` header, containing one or more IPs.  API gateway will append to the end of this, so the header becomes:

~~~
X-Forwarded-For: <spoofed-address(es)>, <real-source>, <api-gateway>
~~~

and `REMOTE_ADDR` will be set to the first spoofed address.

What should be returned is `<real-source>`, which you can get via:

~~~
        'REMOTE_ADDR':
            headers.get(u'X-Forwarded-For', '').split(', ')[-2],
~~~

Of course that will fail if for some reason the header is missing or broken, so safer would be:

~~~
ips = headers.get(u'X-Forwarded-For', '').replace(' ','').split(',')
if len(ips) == 1:
    remote_addr = ips[0]
else:
    remote_addr = ips[-2]

...
        'REMOTE_ADDR':
            remote_addr
~~~

Another option is to use `event["requestContext"]["identity"]["sourceIp"]`, although I've not tested whether that is spoofable or not.

issue_title: New filtering of benchmark tests filters out more than it should
issue_body:
I have proposed a modification to filter non-benchmark tests using the `pytest_collect_modifyitems()` hook instead of  `pytest_runtest_call()` (#84). That modification introduced:

- **a bug:** the filtering is finishing too early because of identation problem in the last lines of the filtering function.
- **a conceptual issue:** previous to the modification, the fixtures were instantiated and  the class of the `benchmark` fixture was checked to ensure that the parameter was indeed a benchmark fixture and not just something else called "benchmark". Now the filtering happens before fixtures are instantiated, so we don't have a way to check against their classes. Instead, i proposed to check if there was an argument called `benchmark`, and select the test if so.

I will provide a fix for the bug, and would like to discuss a modification to the conceptual issue.

I have a use case where I create a bunch of other fixtures that use the `benchmark` fixture and modify its behaviour. In this case, if my test functions receive something like `modified_benchmark` they will not be selected if the check is against the exact string of the parameter name.

One simple possibility is to check if there's a test argument name containing `benchmark` as a substring. This would select all tests that have some parameter matching `.*benchmark.*`. Does that make sense? Is there a better way to do it?

issue_title: "%h" format string are sometimes resolved twice
issue_body: Example in #100

I've figured out how to get the list of SRS-X88 sources. You have to get the list for each scheme in turn. First get the schemes:

```
songpal --endpoint http://192.168.1.18:54480/sony command avContent getSchemeList
Calling avContent.getSchemeList with params None
INFO:songpal.protocol:Calling avContent.getSchemeList(None)
[{'scheme': 'storage'}, {'scheme': 'extInput'}, {'scheme': 'dlna'}]

```
For each scheme, get the source list:
```
songpal --endpoint http://192.168.1.18:54480/sony command avContent getSourceList '{ "scheme": "storage" }'
Calling avContent.getSourceList with params {'scheme': 'storage'}
INFO:songpal.protocol:Calling avContent.getSourceList({'scheme': 'storage'})
[{'playAction': 'changeSource', 'isBrowsable': True, 'meta': 'meta:storage:usb', 'isPlayable': True, 'source': 'storage:usb1', 'title': 'USB'}]

songpal --endpoint http://192.168.1.18:54480/sony command avContent getSourceList '{ "scheme": "extInput" }'
Calling avContent.getSourceList with params {'scheme': 'extInput'}
INFO:songpal.protocol:Calling avContent.getSourceList({'scheme': 'extInput'})
[{'isBrowsable': False, 'meta': 'meta:usbdac', 'title': 'USB DAC', 'source': 'extInput:usbDac', 'isPlayable': True, 'playAction': 'startPlay'}, {'isBrowsable': False, 'meta': 'meta:btaudio', 'title': 'Bluetooth Audio', 'source': 'extInput:btAudio', 'isPlayable': True, 'playAction': 'startPlay'}, {'isBrowsable': True, 'meta': 'meta:line', 'title': 'Audio in', 'source': 'extInput:line', 'isPlayable': False, 'playAction': 'startPlay'}]

songpal --endpoint http://192.168.1.18:54480/sony command avContent getSourceList '{ "scheme": "dlna" }'
Calling avContent.getSourceList with params {'scheme': 'dlna'}
INFO:songpal.protocol:Calling avContent.getSourceList({'scheme': 'dlna'})
[{'isBrowsable': False, 'playAction': 'startPlay', 'title': 'Home Network', 'isPlayable': True, 'source': 'dlna:music', 'meta': 'meta:dlna:music'}]
```
The next problem is that the network option can be set using the "Home Network" or dlna:music URL but the playing source does not match:
```
songpal --endpoint http://192.168.1.18:54480/sony command avContent getPlayingContentInfo {""}
Calling avContent.getPlayingContentInfo with params {}
INFO:songpal.protocol:Calling avContent.getPlayingContentInfo({})
[{'contentKind': 'service', 'parentUri': '', 'stateInfo': {'supplement': '', 'state': 'STOPPED'}, 'service': 'netService:audio?service=spotify', 'uri': 'netService:audio?service=spotify&contentId=-1', 'fileNo': '255', 'source': 'netService:audio'}]
```

So that shows which network service is in use, but they are not available as sources. Spotify is the default, by the way, I've never used it. It could also be cast:audio when casting.

I can't think of any way round that other than a sort of bodge where "Home Network" is treated as the source for any netService, at least for the purposes of source selection.

That said, if you cast to the Sony it automatically switches to network input even if another input is currently active so maybe it doesn't need to be explicitly listed in the inputs.

issue_body:
https://github.com/codingjoe/django-stdimage/blob/master/stdimage/utils.py#L17
In this code part
```python
def __call__(self, instance, filename):
    defaults = {
        'ext': filename.rsplit('.', 1)[1],
        'name': filename.rsplit('.', 1)[0],
        'path': filename.rsplit('/', 1)[0],
        'class_name': instance.__class__.__name__,
    }
    defaults.update(self.kwargs)
    return os.path.join(self.path_pattern % defaults, self.file_pattern % defaults).lower()
```

issue_title: Temporary workspace for operators can only be allocated once
CRF weights never updated in Bi-LSTM-CRF
issue_body:
When using `incubator-mxnet/example/gluon/lstm_crf.py`, CRF transition matrix weights are never updated during training, defeating the purpose of the CRF layer. Printing `model.transitions.data()` each epoch confirmed this.

Compare these lines of MXNet [version](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/example/gluon/lstm_crf.py) and the PyTorch [reference](https://pytorch.org/tutorials/beginner/nlp/advanced_tutorial.html):
```
self.transitions = nd.random.normal(shape=(self.tagset_size, self.tagset_size)) # MXNet
self.transitions = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(self.tagset_size, self.tagset_size)) # PyTorch
```

I was able to solve this issue by changing the above line to:
```
self.transitions = gluon.Parameter("crf_transition_matrix", 
    shape=(self.tagset_size, self.tagset_size))
```
Making this change required adding .data() to all other references to `self.transitions` in the code, eg:
```
self.transitions[next_tag].reshape((1, -1) # Before
self.transitions.data()[next_tag].reshape((1, -1) # After
```
and manually updating the parameter dictionary outside of the class before model initialization:
```
model.params.update({'crf_transition_matrix':model.transitions}) # Added this line
model.initialize(mx.init.Xavier(magnitude=2.24), ctx=mx.cpu())
optimizer = gluon.Trainer(model.collect_params(), 'sgd', {'learning_rate': 0.01, 'wd': 1e-4})
```

issue_title: glTF 2.0 meshes without indices result in empty meshes
issue_body:
The [TriangleWithoutIndices.gltf](https://github.com/KhronosGroup/glTF-Sample-Models/blob/master/2.0/TriangleWithoutIndices/glTF-Embedded/TriangleWithoutIndices.gltf) model demonstrates this issue. To reproduce grab the file and run it through the assimp export and observe that no mesh geometry is created in the output.

Meshes which do not have indices result in a glTF::Mesh::Primitive with a null indicies property (as it should be).

When glTFImporter::ImportMeshes processes the meshes it has a check for prim.indices and only if it's defined does it create the triangle data. The glTF 2.0 specification states the indices property is optional for primitives and when not present the same semantics as WebGL drawArrays() should be used to construct the mesh. Currently if prim.indices is not defined nothing gets made and mNumFaces end up as 0 and you have an empty mesh but with all of the vertex, normal etc data defined.

Willing to have a go at addressing this but wanted to log an issue first and see if others had already looked at it or if my interpretation may be wrong? Looks like a fall through case is needed that makes the mesh in the same way that drawArrays would when prim.indices is not defined, but I'll need to investigate further.

My guess is someone already looked at this and it's not as straight forward as I am assuming so any comments would be appreciated.

I didn't think I would encounter much non-indexed glTF mesh data in the wild but I was just handed two files with exactly that issue and half the geometry is missing as a result so seems it's an important case to handle.

issue_title: Credentials files are only read once and are never reread
issue_body:
We require short term credentials to be used for all API calls. As an easy way to deal with this, we have been writing credentials to a credentials file continually every X minutes and relying on the default search order for finding credentials. That way, we don't have to write any specific authentication code inside the app.

This worked with boto because every time credentials were required, boto went and read from the credentials file. However with boto3/botocore the credentials are only read when the session is created. When the credentials expire boto3/botocore continues to try to use them and we see a RequestExpired error.

Can we make the Credentials object a proxy object to read from file each time they are needed for file credentials?

P.S. I think the same issue exists for environment variables as well although they are less likely to be used in this manner.

issue_title: Parsing bad date strings crashes pypy interpreter
issue_body:
Nice job on the library guys. I'm not sure if this is an issue with pypy or with the library. If you pass in a bad string, this library crashes the interpreter entirely:

    $ pypy
    Python 2.7.9 (295ee98b69288471b0fcf2e0ede82ce5209eb90b, Jun 02 2015, 18:26:45)
    [PyPy 2.6.0 with GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 6.1.0 (clang-602.0.53)] on darwin
    Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
    >>>> import ciso8601
    >>>> ciso8601.parse_datetime_unaware('not-a-date')
    pypy(36682,0x7fff7b7db300) malloc: *** error for object 0x1016df220: pointer being freed was not allocated
    *** set a breakpoint in malloc_error_break to debug
    Abort trap: 6

FWIW normal python 2.7.10 doesn't have the same problem:

    $ python
    Python 2.7.10 (default, Jul 22 2015, 21:18:21) 
    [GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 6.1.0 (clang-602.0.53)] on darwin
    Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
    >>> import ciso8601
    >>> ciso8601.parse_datetime_unaware('not-a-date')
    >>> 

Thanks

issue_title: How can i say for fuzzywuzzy about installed dependencies?
issue_body:
// Excuse my beginner's English

Hi!

How can i explicitly resolve dependencies (show they for `fuzzywuzzy`)?
I have installed `python-Levenshtein` (by command `aptitude install python-Levenshtein`).
And i don't understand have i `difflib` or no.

Any way, `fuzzywuzzy` prints warning:
```
/tmp/fuzzywuzzy/fuzzywuzzy/fuzz.py:33: UserWarning: Using slow pure-python SequenceMatcher
  warnings.warn('Using slow pure-python SequenceMatcher')
```

I have been watching sources, but not figured out what happened...

### My code:
```python
#! /usr/bin/env python3

import sys
import re
sys.path.append('/tmp/fuzzywuzzy')
from fuzzywuzzy import fuzz
from fuzzywuzzy import process

str1 = sys.argv[1]
str2 = sys.argv[2]
print(fuzz.ratio(str1, str2))
```

I run this script like this: `./fuzzy-compare-two-strings.py 123456 213456`

> n.b. i use Debian7

Sorry again and thanks!

Talon requires Numpy 1.6.1, while the latest version is 1.9.2. Version 1.6.1 doesn't install on Mac OS X, failing with this error: `clang: error: invalid argument '-faltivec' only allowed with 'ppc/ppc64/ppc64le'`

This likely already works, but hasn't yet been tested and documented.

The agent should be easily deployable on Heroku.

### Checklist:

* [ ] Check the external binary works ok (download, launching)
* [ ] Check communication between Python & core-agent works (unix sockets)
* [ ] Check that the current extension works (env vars, setting up orgs on APM's site, single sign on)
* [ ] Documentation

issue_title: Support holding shift and control when using `Click Element`
Document how to extend the library
issue_body: The new architecture in SL 3.0 (#771) is likely to break lot of external libraries using SL internally somehow. Explicitly exposing and documenting the public API (#882) helps, but we should still document different approaches how to interact with the library and how to safely extend it. Examples are going to be very valuable.

pipenv seems to use virtualenv internally.
There are several known problems with virtualenv using its own `site.py` as opposed to Python's one, see https://github.com/pypa/virtualenv/issues/355
In particular this means that Python 3 tab completion is not enabled by default.

A simple workaround (unsure how simple really) would be to use python's `venv` module in Python 3

issue_title: Django commands work with `pipenv run`, but not in `pipenv shell` activated environment
issue_body:
I believe this is a unique concern after checking through open issues and reviewing the [diagnose documentation](https://docs.pipenv.org/diagnose/), including the [No module named <module name>](https://docs.pipenv.org/diagnose/#no-module-named-module-name) section. 

I have `pipenv 2018.7.1` (latest) installed according to the [Pragmatic Installation of Pipenv](https://docs.pipenv.org/install/#pragmatic-installation-of-pipenv) instructions in a `debian/contrib-stretch64` Vagrant VM running Python 3.7 (same issue in 3.6 as well). Django version `2.0.7` (latest) is installed with pipenv.

##### Issue description

I am able to run Django commands using `pipenv run python manage.py ...`, but with `pipenv shell` a `ModuleNotFoundError` exception is thrown.

`pipenv run <command>` and `pipenv shell` + `<command>` should produce the same result.

```

Hi.
I would like to report some issue. I'm running Python 3.6.4 v on macOS High Sierra 10.13.3 and everytime i create new folder and try to 
> pipenv install django 
i got this: 
>Warning: the environment variable LANG is not set!
We recommend setting this in ~/.profile (or equivalent) for proper expected behavior.
Creating a virtualenv for this project…
Using /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/bin/python3.6 (3.6.4) to create virtualenv…
blablabla....
    'python_version': python_version(required_python)[: len('2.7')]
TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable

This issue was mentioned here but i didn't find a solution that worked for me tho. So for everyone who faced this problem. Just simply put this in your terminal
> export LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
export LANG=en_US.UTF-8

Then u can install django and use shell.
Have a nice day.

linux-64-tensorflow-0.7.1-py35_1.tar.bz2 is consistent with the

The wheel references the following external versioned symbols in

GLIBCXX_3.4.19.

This constrains the platform tag to "linux_x86_64". In order to
achieve a more compatible tag, you would to recompile a new wheel from
source on a system with earlier versions of these libraries, such as

The following external shared libraries are required by the wheel:

issue_title: Inconsistent dependency from install_scripts to build_scripts's arguments
issue_body:
It is possible to specify the Python executable for generated console scripts by doing:

```
python setup.py build_scripts --executable /ASDFQWER/python install_scripts
```

However this does not work:

```sh
python setup.py build_scripts --executable /ASDFQWER/python
python setup.py install_scripts
```

# How to reproduce
```sh
mkdir foobar
cat >foobar/baz.py <<EOF
def main():
    print 'HELLO WORLD'
EOF
cat >setup.py <<EOF
import setuptools

setuptools.setup(
    packages=["foobar"],
    entry_points = {'console_scripts': ["baz=foobar.baz:main"]}
)
EOF

python setup.py build_scripts --executable=/ASDFQWER
python setup.py install_scripts --install-dir /tmp
```

# Expected behaviour

```console
$ head -1 /tmp/baz
#!/ASDFQWER
```

# Actual behaviour

```console
$ head -1 /tmp/baz
#!/ASDFQWER
```

# Workaround

Run both commands at once:
```
python setup.py build_scripts --executable /ASDFQWER/python install_scripts --install-dir /tmp
```

Hello.

I have one issue related to namespace package. The project I am working on [0] is using pkg_resources-style namespace packages in a correct way described by documentation [1].

the process is:
1. During a build of a first subpackage as RPM package setuptools **skip installation** of `<namespace-package>/__init__.py` with message `Skipping installation of /builddir/build/BUILDROOT/python-moksha-common-1.2.5-1.fc27.x86_64/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/moksha/__init__.py (namespace package)`
2. Then I install newly created RPM from the step one.
3. When I try to build a second subpackage as RPM package (which has the first one as a dependency) build fails because the second subpackage needs to import the first one installed from RPM, but the first one doesn't contain __init__.py file and that is the reason why the namespace logic fails and the other sub-packages is not importable.

In docs [1] there is:
> Every distribution that uses the namespace package must include an identical `__init__.py`. If any distribution does not, it will cause the namespace logic to fail and the other sub-packages will not be importable. Any additional code in `__init__.py` will be inaccessible.

The question is: Why setuptools skip installation of `pkg_resources-style __init__.py` file in namespace package when it must be included in every part of a namespace package?

My current workaround is to add missing files to RPM package manually and then everything works. I also tried to delete `__init__.py` files from the second subpackage before running tests with `python setup.py test` (where import of the first subpackage is needed) but then it fails with `error in moksha.hub setup command: Distribution contains no modules or packages for namespace package 'moksha'`.

I thought that the problem comes from Python 3.6 (bug report with more details and examples [2]) but it seems that Python 3.6 is only more strict in requiring `__init__.py` files everywhere as described in docs.

Maybe I just misunderstood something.

Thank you for help and have a nice day.

[0] https://github.com/mokshaproject/moksha
[1] https://packaging.python.org/guides/packaging-namespace-packages/#pkg-resources-style-namespace-packages
[2] http://bugs.python.org/issue29144
nah, let's just remove the dependencies as per https://github.com/statgen/pheweb/issues/33

issue_title: js-yaml doesn't seem to like map keys with line breaks
issue_body:
According to yamllint.com (and to the spec as far as I understand it) this is valid YAML:
```
foo
bar: |
  baz
```
The js-yaml online demo can't parse that however. 
```
JS-YAML: end of the stream or a document separator is expected at line 2, column 4:
    bar: |
       ^
```
Something like the following is where I'm coming from:
```
- really very long text for a map key where one could reasonably expect 
  a line-break:
    - some more stuff
    - and yet more stuff which is also very long-winded and requires a
      line-break
```
which generates:
```
JS-YAML: bad indentation of a sequence entry at line 2, column 15:
      a line-break:
                  ^
```
Note that the line break within the list item is parsed correctly:
```
- really very long text for a map key where one could reasonably expect a line-break:
    - some more stuff
    - and yet more stuff which is also very long-winded and requires a
      line-break
```
```
[ { 'really very long text for a map key where one could reasonably expect a line-break': 
     [ 'some more stuff',
       'and yet more stuff which is also very long-winded and requires a line-break' ] } ]
```

Often I find that I want to be able to load YAML files even if they contain unrecognized tags. Currently, attempting to do so will result in an error, although I'm not sure why that should be the case. Instead of throwing an error for an unrecognized tag, it seems perfectly reasonable to issue a warning and simply return a raw data structure rather than a constructed instance of some type.

The change log says that warnings were changed to errors for unrecognized tags in version 3.4.0. What was the reasoning for this? Are you willing to revert to the old behavior? A better solution might be to add an optional argument to the `load*` functions that controls whether unrecognized tags cause warnings or errors.

If this seems like a reasonable approach, I am potentially willing to submit a PR with the necessary changes.

The functionality to fetch offsets using time stamps is coded in the `Topic` class method: `fetch_offset_limits`. But I am having problems with fetching the offsets. In principle, this method should return a list of offsets that were written at a timestamp.

See this test code snippet below:

```
client = KafkaClient(broker_version="0.11.0")
topic = client.topics['mytopic']
p = topic.get_producer()
tm = long(time.time() * 1000)
for m_id in range(0,10):
        p.produce('-%d-' % m_id, timestamp = tm, partition_key='k/e/y')
p.stop()

# The following should give me a list of 10 offsets
print topic.fetch_offset_limits(tm, 10)
```
The last line prints:
```
{0: OffsetPartitionResponse(offset=[], err=0)}
```
This `offset` list should have been of length 10, but is empty.

FYI: I tried `kafka-python` consumer method `offset_for_time` and it works fine in this test.

Can you please help me with this.

Thanks.

`
from streamparse import Spout
from config import *
import nsq
import Queue

class NgxLogSpout(Spout):

issue_title: Connections::createSynapse doesn't properly account for destroyed synapses
issue_body:
See https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/blob/44cbf76c754ee7c5a882676de7e565cd356848b4/src/nupic/algorithms/Connections.cpp#L86

Two big problems:

1. It's checking the size of `synapses` which often contains destroyed synapses. It needs to subtract the number of destroyed synapses from this count. Otherwise it will destroy synapses when it doesn't need to.
2. It's destroying synapses and not reusing the index of the newly destroyed synapse. So it will just keep growing the vector.

Basically the code is assuming that `destroySynapse` removes the synapse from the vector. But it doesn't, because that would invalidate everybody's `SynapseIdx`s.

issue_title: Use oauth 'state' parameter for CSRF protection
issue_body:
I didn't follow all the details of how the oauth `state` parameter is currently being used here, so this may be covered already.  But I wanted to make sure we sanity-check all the edge cases.

In addition to tunnelling state through the oauth dance, one of the purposes of the `state` paramter in OAuth is to provide CSRF protection by matching incoming redirects to a particular browser session.

This typically means doing something like:

  * when starting the dance, generate a nonce and set it in both a client session cookie and the outgoing `state` parameter.
  * when receiving a redirect that completes the dance, pull the nonce out of the session cookie and check that it matches the one in the returned `state` parameter.

Without this check, there are scenarios where I can trick you into completing an oauth flow with your user account but my state token, or my user account and your state token, and hence gain unauthorized access.

Some background resources on the topic:

  http://www.thread-safe.com/2014/05/the-correct-use-of-state-parameter-in.html
  http://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-bradley-oauth-jwt-encoded-state-00

issue_title: Terminal content not re-scaling to plugin size
issue_body:
With the latest Qt 5.8.x and latest version of Spyder the terminal doesn't change its size to fit the window it is in. It throws this error while loading (which might just be the case that it is not loading properly):

    [...]
    spyder_terminal/widgets/terminalgui.py", line 50, in __init__
        self.view.page().contentsChanged.connect(self.contents_modified)
       AttributeError: 'WebPage' object has no attribute 'contentsChanged'

After chasing the error through the code, it seems it has to do with the Qt version. From the [Qt documentation](http://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qwebenginepage.html#signals) the signal from `QWebEnginePage`, `contentsChanged` does no longer exist but another one `contentsSizeChanged` does exist.

I've changed [Line 50 of terminalgui.py](https://github.com/spyder-ide/spyder-terminal/blob/master/spyder_terminal/widgets/terminalgui.py#L50) from

    self.view.page().contentsChanged.connect(self.contents_modified)

to:

    self.view.page().contentsSizeChanged.connect(self.contents_modified)

and it works fine now. I'm not even sure if it is what it is supposed to do. But either commenting or changing the line above seems to let the WebView load properly and to help it rendering the terminal full size. If not it crashes and it can't load properly.
So it can be easily applied to a whole function.

This is an issue that should be addressed before we tag v1.3:

It seems that there are some numerical inaccuracies in the rendering of PSFs in the WFIRST module, apparently related to the default Fourier-space sampling.  As I understand it, the Fourier-space sampling for optical PSFs is chosen based on the obscured Airy, but if there are struts then one might need better sampling.  Some tests seem to confirm that the sampling is adequate if we remove the struts, and problems only arise with struts.  The fix for this problem differs depending on whether approximate struts are used or the full pupil plane image is used.

I will put some more plots and comments soon, but I'm mainly opening this not terribly informative issue now to flag the fact that there is one more thing to do before tagging v1.3.

Hi, when try to bootstrap a second postgres instance to join the existing pg cluster (has only 1 node), patroni complains `2017-12-12 21:16:01,073 CRITICAL: system ID mismatch, node postgresql2 belongs to a different cluster: 6497216458191333666 != 6497220080226496012`

Here is our postgres.yml:
```
dcs_api: 'consul://x.x.x.x:8500'
namespace: /service/
scope: example
name: postgresql1 # (1, 2, 3 here for different nodes)

restapi:
  listen: <eth0_ip>:8008    # rest api should be accessible remotely or locally?

consul:
  host: x.x.x.x
  port: 8500

bootstrap:
  dcs:
    ttl: 30
    loop_wait: 10
    retry_timeout: 10
    maximum_lag_on_failover: 1048576
    postgresql:
      use_pg_rewind: true
      parameters:
        max_connections: 500
        shared_buffers: 1GB
        huge_pages: off
        temp_buffers: 128MB
        work_mem: 16MB
        maintenance_work_mem: 256MB
        dynamic_shared_memory_type: posix
        vacuum_cost_limit: 2000
        bgwriter_delay: 20ms
        effective_io_concurrency: 2
        wal_level: logical
        checkpoint_timeout: 15min
        max_wal_size: 50GB
        min_wal_size: 1GB
        checkpoint_completion_target: 0.8
        archive_mode: on
        archive_command: '/bin/true'
        max_wal_senders: 8
        max_replication_slots: 8
        track_commit_timestamp: on
        hot_standby: on
        hot_standby_feedback: on
        random_page_cost: 1.2
        effective_cache_size: 2GB
        default_statistics_target: 1000
        logging_collector: on
        max_locks_per_transaction: 256
        hba_file: /etc/postgresql/9.6/main/pg_hba.conf
        ident_file: /etc/postgresql/9.6/main/pg_ident.conf

  initdb:
    - encoding: UTF8

  pg_hba:
    - local   all             postgres                                  peer
    - local   all             all                                       trust
    - host    all             all             10.0.0.0/8                md5
    - host    replication     repuser         10.0.0.0/8                md5

postgresql:
  listen: <local_eth0_ip>:5432
  connect_address: <eth0_ip>:5432
  data_dir: /var/lib/postgresql/9.6/main
  pgpass: /tmp/pgpass0
  authentication:
    superuser:
      username: postgres
      password: pg_password
    replication:
      username: repuser
      password: rp_password
  parameters:
    unix_socket_directories: '.'
  custom_conf: /etc/postgresql/9.6/main/postgresql.conf

  nofailover: false

  nosync: false
```

I understand there are some parameters not needed for the configuration however we found a few issues:
1. 1.3.6 of Patroni won't honor some config such as `max_locks_per_transaction: 256` so we had to change https://github.com/zalando/patroni/blob/master/patroni/postgresql.py#L217 to ` parameters = config['parameter'].copy()`. Otherwise postgresql won't start.
2. Once postgres1 is setup, using the same yml configuration for postgres2 and execute `patroni /etc/patroni/postgres.yml` will lead to `system ID mismatch` error.

issue_title: 'table.insert_data()' frequently fails in the Python BiqQuery client with error: [Errno 32] Broken pipe

issue_title: [BigTable] Insert new record with the existing row_key multiple times?
issue_body:
Is it possible to append additional rows with the existing 'rowkey' in Bigtable?

it seems when I call 

```python
from operator import itemgetter
import random

def get_row_key(record, template):
    attrs = itemgetter(*template)
    vals = attrs(record)
    return '#'.join((str(v) for v in vals)

# main
record = dict(
    user=random.randint(10, 1000),
    viewability=30,
    developer=1,
    advertiser=2,
    campaign=3,
    game=4,
    spot=5,
    asset=6,
    sex='m',
    age=50,
    browser='chrome',
    browser_ver='55',
    os='android',
    os_ver='5.0',
    mobile=True,
    desktop=False,
    country='us',
    ip_addr='127.0.0.1',
    created=1478218018,
    tick_size=5,
    session='aaaa'
    is_generic=False
)

# pattern used in itemgetter
rowkey = ('asset', 'created', 'advertiser')
row = table.row(get_row_key(record, pattern))
for column, val in record.items():
    row.set_cell('stat', column, val)
row.commit()
```

so if I run code against the same record 3 times (which will produce the same rowkey for the row) I only get a single row. It seems 1st row is inserted, and then 2nd and 3rd mutate the same row. How to make it append? (OR row_key HAS TO BE UNIQUE?)

thanks,
Dmitry

issue_title: 今天连不上了，[Errno 104] Connection reset by peer
issue_body:
之前一直好用，今天突然连不上了，
错误提示，[Errno 104] Connection reset by peer
请问怎么破？

Ref: https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/cloudformation-templates-us-west-2/EC2InstanceWithSecurityGroupSample.template

Invalid format generated:
```json
"SecurityGroupIngress": [
                    {
                        "Properties": {
                            "CidrIp": "0.0.0.0/0",
                            "FromPort": "0",
                            "IpProtocol": "-1",
                            "ToPort": "65535"
                        },
                        "Type": "AWS::EC2::SecurityGroupIngress"
                    }
                ]
```

With the above template AWS will complain:
```
Encountered unsupported property Type
```

Correct format:
```json
"SecurityGroupIngress": [
                    {
                        "CidrIp": "0.0.0.0/0",
                        "FromPort": "0",
                        "IpProtocol": "-1",
                        "ToPort": "65535"
                    }
                ]
```

[AWS::ElastiCache::ReplicationGroup](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-elasticache-replicationgroup.html)

Use the `AtRestEncryptionEnabled` property to enable encryption at rest.

Use the `AuthToken` property to specify a password that's used to access a password-protected server.

Use the `TransitEncryptionEnabled` property to enable in-transit encryption.

issue_title: lr_scheduler seems to be reset if training is resumed
issue_body:
If training is resumed the learning rate scheduler seems to be rerest.
This seems to be the case because the class OptimizerConfig contains the learning rate scheduler both directly in lr_scheduler and also in the dict params, and the module.init_optimizer function relies on the params dict but not on OptimizerConfig.lr_scheduler.

https://github.com/awslabs/sockeye/blob/da518f1c6e90e08e2a1f8d2888608121f75b3d67/sockeye/training.py#L991
       # (6) Learning rate scheduler
       with open(os.path.join(self.training_state_dirname, C.SCHEDULER_STATE_NAME), "rb") as fp:
            self.optimizer_config.lr_scheduler = pickle.load(fp)
        # initialize optimizer again
        self.model.initialize_optimizer(self.optimizer_config)

Quick fix might be adding
         self.optimizer_config.params['lr_scheduler'] = self.optimizer_config.lr_scheduler
before
    self.model.initialize_optimizer()

But overall it looks dangerous to have the learning rate scheduler both in lr_scheduler and in the dict params within the class OptimizerConfig.

issue_title: Mark dependency on MxNet as >= 1.2, not == 1.2

issue_title: [Issue on create-rule-template] Role deployed by CFN is not compatible with cross account python template
issue_body:
When the Global variable ASSUME_ROLE_MODE = True, the config role is being assumed by lambda in the scenario of a cross account to put-evaluation, describe, etc. 
The role config-rdk has the correct configuration (trust policy and permission policy)

The !Sub function doesn't seem to work in when used inside the AWS::Serverless::Function Policies property. 

In the following cases I am trying to use the AWS::AccountId pseudo parameter within the policies property.

**Case 1:**
Set Policies value to a single string

```
  lambda:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      ...
      Policies: !Sub arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:policy/my-policy
      ...
```

**Case 2:**
Set Policies value to an array of strings

```
  lambda:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      ...
      Policies: 
        - !Sub arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:policy/my-policy
      ...
```

**Outcome**

The policy is simply ignored. I don't get an error, but the policy is not attached to the generated role.

Without the parameter it works fine.

**Case 3:**
Use the literal value and it works fine

```
  lambda:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      ...
      Policies: 
        - !Sub arn:aws:iam::123456789:policy/my-policy
      ...
```

If the transformations generate pretty basic errors, for example the following "error":

```
  MyLambdaFunction:
    Type: "AWS::Serverless::Function"
    Properties:
      CodeUri: build
      FunctionName: "my-function-name"
      Handler: "my_module.handler"
      Runtime: "python3.6"
      Timeout: "30"
      Role: !ImportValue "mu-lambda-role"
```

Generates the following Transformation error:

```
Resource with id [MyLambdaFunction] is invalid. Type of property 'Timeout' is invalid.
```

It would be great if there errors can be extended with additional information so we can trace the error back to the actual resource/property (or perhaps even the line/column number) of the original SAM template that gave the error.

The transformations and a (more) detailed error output is really valuable for consomers like SAM-CLI (to perform the "validate" function), but also by the (new) CloudFormation Linter (cfn-python-lint).

This would allow consumers of SAM to give better and more direct feedback on errors in the SAM/CloudFormation template and make the usage of SAM easier.

Extending the `InvalidDocumentException` (I think this is the place where it could/should be added) with this information would be really appreciated:

https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/blob/master/samtranslator/model/exceptions.py

How solve this problem?

Currently, imageio will automatically download certain files when needed:

* a set of standard images, like `chelsea.png`  and `astronaut.png`.
* the freeimage library
* the avbin library
* the ffmpeg executable

Downloading of standard images should stay, in my opinion, but automatic downloading of executables or libraries is a bit curious. We sort of needed it to make the freeimage plugin Just Work, which provides the majority of formats, but now that we rely on Pillow (#167) this is much less of a problem.

In #167, the automatic downloading of libraries and executable is therefore removed. Instead an exception will be raised that displays a message of a function to call to explicitly download the exe/lib, e.g. `imageio.plugins.ffmpeg.download()`.

I think this is ok for FreeImage, and avbin is sort of redundant with ffmpeg anyway, but it makes the ffmpeg plugin not just work. 

@Zulko will probably want to be aware of this. The exceptions mentioned above are `imageio.core.NeedDownloadError` instances, so you can catch them specifically. 

For people using conda to install Moviepy we can make it easy. Since ffmpeg is available as a conda package (on conda-forge), maybe imageio should be made to be able to make use of it (if it not already does), and Moviepy's conda package can add it as a dependency.

issue_title: 'charm login' requests my password, promises to be good, violates oauth
issue_body:
Rather than redirect me to the SSO website where I can enter my password securely, 'charm login' instead requests I enter my password and posts it on my behalf to the SSO website. This requires me to trust charm-tools with my password, and the libraries it uses to correctly verify the chain of trust and refuse to continue if the SSL certificate is not valid (MITM attacks), and does not give me an opportunity to confirm the access level being requested. These are all reasons that the oauth protocol exists in the first place ("OAuth is an authentication protocol that allows users to approve application to act on their behalf without sharing their password.")

What should happen is it opens a browser window to the SSO website where I can login if necessary, do the 2fa dance if necessary, and confirm that I wish to grant the requested access.

issue_title: deisctl: Print proper error message when getting status of global units
issue_body:
Some of the Deis components, notably: `store-monitor`, `store-daemon`, `store-metadata`, `store-volume`, `logspout`, and `publisher`are fleet global units. This means that we schedule them once to fleet, and fleet will ensure they run on all hosts in the cluster. 

The output of `deisctl status` on a global unit is confusing: `Unit deis-store-volume.service does not appear to be running.`. This is because the status of global units cannot be queried in this way. We should detect this in deisctl and provide appropriate output so as not to confuse users.

issue_title: Rename egg-info directories for `noarch: python` packages
issue_body:
If a package was built with an older `pip <10` and `setuptools`, it can include Python version-specific `egg-info` directories, e.g., `site-packages/overrides-1.9-py2.7.egg-info` in `conda-forge::overrides=1.9=py_0`.
If those packages are `noarch: python` ones, `conda` will install those into the environment's `python`'s site-packages directory but not adjust their names, i.e., the above example will be installed into a `python=3.6` environment as
```
lib/python3.6/site-packages/overrides-1.9-py2.7.egg-info/
```
rather than
```
lib/python3.6/site-packages/overrides-1.9-py3.6.egg-info/
```
This could cause issues when distribution info like entry points are queried.

Surely it makes sense to build packages to rather use `dist-info` instead of `egg-info`. But we could also teach `conda` to rename those directories for `noarch: python` packages.

I'm not aware that a `-py2.7.egg-info` can include Python 2.7-specific things, so I think simply renaming them would be fine. But if anyone as more in-depth knowledge of the contents of `.egg-info`, please feel free to correct or confirm that assumption!

xref: https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/3055#issuecomment-409324132

issue_body:
I created a package using `conda build mypackage` successfully and I can install the local version just fine to a conda enviornment  `conda install -n testenv  --use-local /anacondapath/anaconda/conda-bld/linux-64/example.tar.bz2` 

However when I specify an exact path using -p, only the directory name of my module is placed in the enviroment's site-packages, excluding \__init__.py and other modules making it unaccessible. eg. `conda install -p /pathto/myother/environment/testenv  --use-local /anacondapath/anaconda/conda-bld/linux-64/example.tar.bz2`. I want to do this to test the build before uploading to binstar and want to keep anaconda's global environments clean because multiple users use the same root environment.

$ conda info:
platform : linux-64
conda version : 3.11.0
conda-build version : 1.12.1
python version : 2.7.9.final.0

The file `info/package_metadata.json` should be renamed to something more meaningful.  The current name is not useful, because all content of `info/` is "package metadata".  Moreover the name is extra log.  Calling the file `info/foo.json` would at least be shorted, although it wouldn't mean anything either.  Filenames like this one, are part of the external API on conda, and choosing meaningful names reflects on the project as a whole.

CC @kalefranz, @msarahan, @csoja

issue_title: Consider using patterns for exception filter
issue_body:
We have two settings to filter events: `filter_exception_types` and `transactions_ignore_patterns`. The former uses exact string matching on the class/module name of the exception, the latter uses a regex match against the transaction name.

This is somewhat confusing. Ideally, we use regex for both filters.

There is a timeout exception on random commands.
We rollback on the version 1.1.1 of the python api and stay on the same elasticsearch version (1.3.7)

Best regards,
Damien

issue_body:

The subscription is not registered for the resource type 'managedClusters' in the location 'westus'. Please re-register for this provider in order to have access to this location.
```

### Description
Microsoft.Network is releasing new feature called “virtual network private access” in the fist week of August. There're 2 Microsoft.Network API changes in this feature: 
1. New subnet.properties.privateAccessServices property
2. New subscription-wide API GET /privateAccessServices
We need your help to enable these in CLIv2. See details below. 

See CLIv1 info here: 
"https://microsoft.sharepoint.com/teams/WAG/AzureNetworking/Engineering H  P/Network Resource Provider/Design/Service tunnel powershell changes.docx?web=1" 

Evgeny Agafonchikov (Akvelon) <v-evgena@microsoft.com> will send out swagger PR for these changes tomorrow

We discussed this over email with:
Mayuri Diwan <mayurid@microsoft.com>; 
Travis Prescott <trpresco@microsoft.com>; 
Jason Shaver <Jason.Shaver@microsoft.com>

1. PrivateAccessServices is a new property of a subnet: it’s an array of objects, each object has 1 property – “service” on input and 3 properties 

on output. 
It’s updated as part of PUT subnet or PUT virtualNetwork (subnet is a child of the virtual network)
INPUT (under subnet.properties in PUT subnet and PUT vnet requests)
         "privateAccessServices": [ 
              {
                    "service": “Microsoft.Storage”
                },
                { 
                    "service": “Microsoft.Sql”
                }
            ],
OUTPUT:
            "privateAccessServices": [ 
               { 
                    "provisioningState": “Updating|Deleting|Failed|Succeeded”,
                    "service": “Microsoft.Storage”,
                    "locations": ["eastus", "westus"]
                },
                { 
                    "provisioningState": “Updating|Deleting|Failed|Succeeded”,
                    "service": “Microsoft.Sql”,
                    "locations": ["eastus"]
                }
            ],
Minimum required api-version for the new property is 2016-12-01
PS spec Service tunnel powershell changes.docx?web=1
Feature spec: Storage account and SQL DB ACLs - NRP design.docx?web=1

2. We also need CLIv2 support for this subscription-wide GET API:
INPUT
GET /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/providers/Microsoft.Network/locations/{location}/privateAccessServices?api-version=2017-04-01
OUTPUT:
{
  "value": [
    {
      "id": "/subscriptions/s1/providers/Microsoft.Network/privateAccessServices/Microsoft.Storage",
      "name": "Microsoft.Storage",
      "type": "Microsoft.Network/privateAccessServices"
    },
    {
      "id": "/subscriptions/s1/providers/Microsoft.Network/privateAccessServices/Microsoft.Sql",
      "name": "Microsoft.Sql",
      "type": "Microsoft.Network/privateAccessServices"
    }
  ]
}
Minimum required api-version for this GET api is 2017-04-01
Note, that output is subscription and location specific. For example, subscriptions that have AllowServiceTunneling flag may see more services in the output and in some regions, there may be no services enabled for private access yet.

Note: Sample ARM template: \\scratch2\scratch\vladsero\vnetWithST.json 
You’ll need a prod subscription with Microsoft.Network/AllowServiceTunneling feature flag to try this (“service tunneling” is old/internal name of the feature). Anitha Adusumilli <Anitha.Adusumilli@microsoft.com> can set it for you.

issue_title: Pre-condition Headers not Supported by Storage Service
issue_body:
During our review with the storage service team, they pointed out that the SDK exposes numerous pre-condition headers that are not in fact supported by the service. If these parameters do nothing, they should probably not be part of the public SDK interface.

Here are some of the SDK calls which expose unsupported pre-condition headers:

- `*BlobService.get_blob_metadata`
- `*BlobService.get_blob_properties`
- `*BlobService.acquire_container_lease`
- `*BlobService.break_container_lease`
- `*BlobService.change_container_lease`
- `*BlobService.release_container_lease`
- `*BlobService.renew_container_lease`
- `*BlobService.set_container_metadata`

There may be more, but those were the ones specifically called out by the storage service team.

CC: @hasonmsft, @vinjiang, @johanste, @mayurid

In http://data.openaddresses.io.s3.amazonaws.com/runs/460/output.txt from sources/us-pa-bucks.json:

```
Error doing excerpt; skipping
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/openaddr/__init__.py", line 154, in conform
    data_sample, geometry_type = task3.excerpt(decompressed_paths, workdir, conform)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/openaddr/conform.py", line 217, in excerpt
    raise ValueError('Not enough rows in data source')
ValueError: Not enough rows in data source
```

It might not be possible to get non-UTF8 strings out of OGR sources with GDAL 1.11.x, based on posts from @NelsonMinar and @rouault:

- https://nelsonslog.wordpress.com/2015/01/15/ogrpython-vs-unicode/
- https://lists.osgeo.org/pipermail/gdal-dev/2016-January/043575.html

This is currently affecting several Italian sources. We'll need `Feature.GetFieldAsBinary` to be present.

issue_title: Allow unsigned types in PHY_ScalarType enum
issue_body:
Bullet should allow more unsigned types in the `PHY_ScalarType` enum. This way It is not possible to use unsigned data in a ´btHeightfieldTerrainShape´ without manually converting it to signed. I understand this might affect bullet in sevaral other places. Is there a plan to add more types in the future?
